cases.description: Basic case-sensitive positional search for the first occurrence
FIND is case-sensitive: lowercase 'a' does not match the uppercase 'A' in "Apple", and there is no other 'a' -> #VALUE! (contrast with SEARCH, which is case-insensitive and would find it)
cases.expected_note: Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-gb/office/find-function-06213f91-b5be-4544-8b0b-2fd5a775436f and https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/how-to-correct-a-value-error-in-find-findb-and-search-searchb-functions-0d1299aa-6234-4253-bc5e-50881b575fc6 - FIND is case-sensitive and does not support wildcard characters

cases.description: find_text not present anywhere in within_text -> #VALUE!
start_num=3 begins the search at position 3, skipping the earlier match of 'p' at position 2
